data.title: Li Auto 2024 Vehicle Margin Dropped Due to Different Product Mix, Changes in Pricing Strategy >LI
Cellectis SA reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cellectis SA reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 8 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -70 cents. The mean expectation of six analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 18 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -37 cents to 6 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 662.1% to $15.17 million from a year ago; analysts expected $9.86 million.

  • Cellectis SA's reported EPS for the quarter was 8 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $5.92 million.

  • Cellectis SA shares had fallen by 28.3% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the biotechnology & medical research peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cellectis SA is $6.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 14 at 08:52 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. (For questions concerning the data in this report, contact Estimates.Support@lseg.com. For any other questions or feedback, contact RefinitivNewsSupport@thomsonreuters.com)

data.title: WeRide up after reporting record-high robotaxi revenue in 2024
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of Chinese autonomous driving technology company WeRide up 5.2% at $17.11 premarket

** WeRide reports record-high robotaxi revenue in 2024

** Company says product revenue in 2024 rose to 87.7 million yuan ($12.13 million) from 54.2 million yuan in 2023, primarily due to a significant increase in the sales of robotaxis, robosweepers and robovans

** WeRide expects the number of robotaxis to reach 50 units in mid-2025 on Uber's platform

** The company's stock went public in the U.S. in October, raising $440.5 million in its IPO and private placement

** Up to last close, stock had risen ~15% so far this year

($1 = 7.2305 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: WeRide Q4 Loss Narrows, Revenue Decreases
data.description:
WeRide reported a Q4 loss Friday of 2.43 Chinese renminbi per diluted American depositary share, narrowing from a loss of 9.60 renminbi a year earlier.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was 140.8 million renminbi, down from 144.8 million renminbi a year earlier.

Comparable analyst estimates were not readily available.

Shares of the Chinese autonomous driving technology firm were up more than 5% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Li Auto Sees 1Q Rev CNY23.4B-CNY24.7B >LI
Li Auto Sees 1Q Deliveries to Between 88,000 Units and 93,000 Units >LI
Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0852 GMT - Bitcoin rises as risk sentiment recovers on optimism that the U.S. could avoid a government shutdown. Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer said he would support a Republican funding bill set for Friday afternoon, removing the threat of a government shutdown. It marks a reversal in his stance after he vowed that he and other Democrats would block the bill. Hopes that a U.S. government shutdown will be averted is boosting risk appetite, helping U.S. stock futures to rise, Tickmill Group's Patrick Munnelly says in a note. IG futures data show the tech-heavy Nasdaq opening 0.9% higher. Bitcoin has a strong positive correlation to the U.S. index. The cryptocurrency is last up 2.6% at $82,467, according to LSEG.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0847 GMT - Bodycote's executed well last year, especially in the second half, amid uncertainty among industrials, Jefferies analysts say. The provider of heat-treatment and thermal-processing services also made good progress on the strategic objectives set out last December, "with plenty more to come, supporting management's confidence in its medium-term targets," they write in a note to investors. Ebita consensus forecasts for 2025 will likely move lower, they warn, but say the potential for improvement remains. Shares are down 10.8% at 5.68 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0840 GMT - BMW investors will focus on the German carmaker's auto EBIT margin guidance of 5%-7%, BMW being the first car manufacturer to take into account the impact of U.S. tariffs in its outlook, Bernstein says in a note. This includes duties up to Wednesday, which account for roughly one percentage point of BMW's auto margin. "Were it not for these, the guidance would have been 6%-8%, which compares to what we reckon to be a buy-side consensus of 6%-7%," Bernstein analysts say. BMW shares trade 4.1% lower at 78.94 euros. (sarah.sloat@wsj.com)

0832 GMT - Bodycote's performance update will likely lead to a 5%-8% cut to consensus estimates for the London-listed group's adjusted Ebita, J.P. Morgan analysts say. This is because the provider of heat-treatment and thermal-processing services warned that it still faces challenging conditions in its automotive and industrial businesses. Structural demand in aerospace and defense remains strong but is affected by industry-wide supply-chain bottlenecks. The group also expects a sequentially flat performance for the first half of this year, the analysts say. Shares drop 8.6% to 5.82 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0830 GMT - Hong Kong shares ended higher amid positive sentiment. Investors anticipate more details on China's consumption stimulus to be announced at a State Council presser Monday. Consumer stocks led session gains, with Li Ning up 6.4% and China Mengniu Dairy rising 9.0%. Gold-mining stocks also climbed as the precious metal surged. Shandong Gold Mining increased 5.5% and Zijin Mining rose 4.9%. CK Hutchison slumped 6.4% after Beijing signaled unhappiness over its agreement to sell its majority controlling stake in Panama ports to a BlackRock-led consortium. The benchmark Hang Seng Index ended 2.1% higher at 23959.98; the Hang Seng Tech Index gained 2.3%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0821 GMT - Portugal's decision not to buy F-35 fighter jets from the U.S. will likely provide a boost for European defense companies, Jefferies analysts say. The country cited the need to take into account "the predictability of our allies" when making defense procurement decisions. The move is slightly negative for BAE Systems, a supplier to the F-35 program, but the U.K. company could still benefit from an equivalent Eurofighter order from Portugal, alongside its Eurofighter consortium partners Leonardo and Airbus, the analysts say. French groups Dassault Aviation, Thales and Safran would benefit if Portugal decides to buy Rafale jets in this upcoming tender, which the analysts estimate could be for around 28 aircraft. BAE Systems is up 0.5%, Leonardo is up 0.4%, Airbus is down 0.1%, Dassault Aviation is up 0.9%, Thales is up 1%, and Safran is up 0.4%. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0819 GMT - Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent downplayed the dollar's fall this year but this is unlikely to indicate much for U.S policy on the currency, ING's Chris Turner says in a note. Bessent said in an interview with CNBC Thursday that the dollar's deprecation is a natural adjustment after last year's appreciation. "We doubt that means much/anything for the U.S. Treasury's dollar policy and he's just acknowledging the noise/disturbance this 'transition' period is having on the U.S. economy." Ultimately the Trump administration would prefer a weaker dollar but a global trade war is positive for the currency, he says. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0759 GMT - Swiss Life delivered solid 2024 results, but the company needs to beat expectations more strongly to justify its high valuation, Vontobel's Simon Foessmeier says in a research note. The Swiss insurer reported results that were broadly as expected, according to Vontobel. After-tax profit was in line with Vontobel's estimate, with fee and commission income slightly below expectations but fees better than the consensus, the analyst says. "While we are confident that management will deliver, the valuation multiples appear elevated. The firm's track record and the dividend--plus share buyback attraction--lead us to maintain our Hold rating," the analyst says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0758 GMT - The stock market selloff is mainly driven by uncertainty around valuations, rather than growth concerns, Danske Bank's Sofie Pedersen says in a note. The U.S.'s largest tech stocks of Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Nvidia, and Tesla--known as the magnificent 7--led declines on Thursday together with defensive real estate stocks, Pedersen says. In contrast, cyclical materials and financials were relative outperformers. "We interpret this as markets making it clear that it is not economic growth that is yet driving the selloff but uncertainty with valuation in focus rather than earnings," Danske says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0756 GMT - Heineken's prospects seem to be improving given the management's prudent expectations, capital allocation and its revenue investment, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. Over the last year, the brewer's growth guidance prudently came down toward around 4%, in line with other brewers, they say. RBC expects Heineken's revenue for 2025-26 to be around 1% ahead of the market consensus and the EBIT margin to remain broadly flat over the next three years as the company continues to invest to boost growth. The analysts reduce capital expenditure forecasts to be more in line with the industry. RBC upgrades the stock's rating to outperform from sector perform and increases the price target to 92 euros from 80 euros. Heineken's shares closed at 77.6 euros. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - China's benchmark Shanghai Composite Index rose 1.8% to 3419.56, its highest close this year. The Shenzhen Composite Index gained 2.05% and the ChiNext Price Index climbed 2.8%. Market sentiment was boosted on hopes that Beijing would move to ease liquidity in the Chinese banking system before the weekend. Sentiment was also supported by hopes that policies to boost consumption could be announced at a press conference held by key Chinese policymakers on Monday. Gold stocks were among advancers, with Chifeng Jilong Gold Mining Co. up 3.8% and Zhongjin Gold Corp. 2.7% higher. Among other winners, China Life Insurance and Ping An Insurance gained 5.8% and 4.6%, respectively. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0708 GMT - Kering's decision to name Demna Gvasalia as the new artistic director of Gucci raises doubts, Bernstein analysts say in a note. The designer will join Gucci from Balenciaga--which is also owned by Kering--in early July. The strategy he implemented at Balenciaga might not work as well for a bigger brand, the analysts say. Kering has been trying to turn Gucci around in an effort to reinvigorate demand for its core brand. "We are not sure that Demna measures up to the task, nor that he is the right fit for Gucci at the moment," Bernstein says. Shares in the French luxury-goods group have fallen nearly 41% in the past 12 months. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0758 GMT - The stock market selloff is mainly driven by uncertainty around valuations, rather than growth concerns, Danske Bank's Sofie Pedersen says in a note. The U.S.'s largest tech stocks of Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, Nvidia, and Tesla--known as the magnificent 7--led declines on Thursday together with defensive real estate stocks, Pedersen says. "We interpret this as markets making it clear that it is not economic growth that is yet driving the selloff but uncertainty with valuation in focus rather than earnings," Danske says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0646 GMT - Beverage industry association spiritsEurope has called on the EU and the U.S. to stop using the sector as a "bargaining chip," amid a trade dispute between the 27-nation bloc and the country. President Trump said on Thursday that he would impose a 200% tariff on U.S. imports of wine, Champagne and other alcoholic beverages from the bloc. Trump said the move was in response to the EU's decision to implement a 50% levy on American whiskey. "SpiritsEUROPE is deeply alarmed by the renewed threat of tariffs on EU and U.S. spirits--once again, as part of an entirely unrelated dispute," the trade group says. "EU spirits companies have invested heavily in U.S. production, including American Whiskey, just as U.S. spirits companies own distilleries across the EU," it says. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Aeroports de Paris Issues EUR1 Billion of Bonds
data.description:
French airport operator Aeroports de Paris completed the issue of a dual-tranche bond worth 1 billion euros on Thursday.

The first tranche of 500 million euros has an annual fixed rate coupon of 3.500% and will mature in March 2033, while the second 500 million-euro tranche carries a 3.750% coupon, with a March 2036 maturity.

Settlement of both bonds will take place on March 20.

data.title: Euro zone bond yields firm as markets gauge German spending plans
data.description:
Euro zone bond yields ticked higher on Friday but remained slightly below multi-month highs, as investors gauged the progress of a parliamentary debate on a huge boost to German spending and the potential impact of U.S. tariffs on Europe.

Germany's 10-year bond yield (DE10YT=RR), the benchmark for the euro zone bloc, rose 2.3 basis points (bps) to 2.88%.

The country's 10-year yield hit its highest since October earlier this week at 2.938% as investors continued to react to Chancellor-in-waiting Friedrich Merz's plans to spend much more on infrastructure and overhaul German debt rules.

Italy's 10-year yield increased by 1.8 basis points​ at 3.96%, and the gap between Italian and German bond yields (DE10IT10=RR) stood at 107 bps.

The spread between U.S. 10-year Treasuries and German bunds (DE10US10=RR) stood at 142 bps after falling below 140 bps earlier this week, its lowest since July 2023.

U.S. bond yields have fallen due to concerns about the impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs on the economy.

Germany's two-year bond yield (DE2YT=RR), which is more sensitive to European Central Bank rate expectations, was up 1.7 bps at 2.2%.

data.title: Stellantis to supply Italy's Iveco with two EV vans for Europe
data.description:
Stellantis and Iveco said on Friday they have agreed for the automaker to supply the Italian truckmaker two fully-electric (EV) van models, helping Iveco further expand its EV van range in Europe.

Sales of Iveco-badged vans produced by Stellantis are expected to start by mid-2026, based on a ten-year supply, the companies said in a joint statement, without providing financial terms of the agreement.

The new vans will be based on Stellantis' mid- and large-sized EV van platforms, the companies added.

They will be produced at Stellantis Pro One van unit's plants in Italy's Atessa, Poland's Gliwice, and France's Hordain and will be distributed by Iveco in Europe, including the United Kingdom, through established channels.

With a gross vehicle weight of 2.8-3.1 tons and 3.5-4.25 tons respectively, "the two future Iveco vans will complement the brand's battery electric offer for the European market and extend it into this lighter weight segment," the companies said.

The deal follows a similar one Iveco struck last year with South Korea's Hyundai Motor for the supply to the Italian group of a mid-sized electric van in the 2.5-3.5 ton weight range, expanding an existing partnership agreement.

Iveco Group is controlled by Exor , the investment company of Italy's Agnelli family, which is also Stellantis' single largest shareholder.

data.title: Gaush Meditech Unit's Imaging Diagnostic Platform Gets Registration Approval in China
data.description:
Gaush Meditech's subsidiary, Heiderberg Engineering, obtained a medical device registration certificate from China for its multimodal imaging diagnostic platform, Anterion.

Anterion supports various ophthalmic specialties by integrating biometry, corneal topography, and anterior segment OCT. This aids in precise refractive and cataract surgery planning through corneal assessment and biometric data, according to a Thursday disclosure with the Hong Kong Exchange.

data.title: Copper Extends Rally to 9-Month High
data.description:
Copper futures rose to above $4.9 per pound on Friday, a nine-month high, and more than $0.4 higher than comparable contracts at the LME on expectations that the US will impose tariffs on copper, a move that would pressure the limited capacity for local smelters.

President Trump signed an executive order to initiate a review on copper imports after noting he would tariff the metal in an earlier speech before Congress.

The signals suggested that levies would be imposed later in the year, but the escalation on base metal tariffs on Canada sporadically announced by the White House ramped up concerns that barriers on copper may be expedited.

Such a move would increase dependency on domestic capacity, which is limited to only two major smelters, as the US imports nearly half of its copper.

In China, supply remained ample.

Treatment charges by smelters were still below zero, reflecting the large extent of overcapacity in refined copper production in the major producer.

data.title: Bitcoin logo shines over Austin, Texas as Gemini sets new world record
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange Gemini set a new Guinness world record by deploying 1,000 drones to form the Bitcoin logo in the sky, marking the largest-ever aerial display of a currency symbol.

The event, held on March 13 in Austin, Texas, celebrated the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve initiative.

Gemini’s drone show featured depictions of a rocket launch and the moon landing, among others. During the show, the Bitcoin (BTC) logo was followed by a text that read “Go where dollars won’t.” The company said:

“In celebration of the US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, we’re hosting a Guinness World Record breaking drone show. The show explores the future of money and features the iconic Bitcoin “₿” as the largest currency symbol in the sky.”

Following the successful drone show, Gemini received a certificate for “The largest ariel display of a currency symbol formed by multirotor/drones.”

The Bitcoin logo origin story

In over 16 years of Bitcoin’s existence, its logo has undergone several iterations driven by community feedback. The first Bitcoin logo, created by Satoshi Nakamoto, represented a gold coin with a “BC” text embedded in the center, as shown below.

However, Nakamoto introduced a new logo on Feb. 24, 2010, which replaced the “BC” text with “₿.”

Satoshi Nakamoto incorporates design changes based on community feedback. Source: bitcointalk.org

The logo was released as a copyright-free image and was widely accepted as the official symbol for Bitcoin. 

On Nov. 1, 2010, a new iteration of the Bitcoin logo was created by a Bitcoin community member bitboy (unrelated to YouTuber BitBoy Crypto), which received overwhelming support from the early Bitcoiners.

As a result, bitboy’s logo was accepted as the official Bitcoin logo and continues to be used to date.

The updated logo replaced the gold background with a bright orange color and featured Nakamoto’s “₿” logo tiled clockwise by 14%.

Read Cointelegraph’s detailed BTC origin story to learn more about the evolution of the Bitcoin logo.

Magazine: Vitalik on AI apocalypse, LA Times both-sides KKK, LLM grooming: AI Eye

data.title: Gold Futures Exceed $3,000 an Ounce on Inflation Data, Economic Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Gold futures rise to new highs on U.S. inflation data and safe-haven demand. Futures are up 0.4% at $3,003.40 a troy ounce, having risen as high as $3,006.70/oz earlier in the session. Modest U.S. inflation numbers have boosted hopes the Federal Reserve will ease monetary policy, ING analysts say in a note. The market has priced in another 25 basis point reduction in June, with around 70bps of easing expected for the full year, ING writes. Lower interest rates typically boost the appeal of non-interest bearing bullion. Gold's rally has also been fuelled by concerns that tariffs will boost inflation and slow economic growth, prompting investors to search for safe-haven assets, ING adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: REG-Issue of Equity
data.description:
Foresight VCT PLC

LEI: 213800GNTY699WHACF46

Issue of Equity

Allotment of Ordinary Shares

14 March 2025

The Board of Foresight VCT plc (“the Company”) is pleased to announce that 101,337 Ordinary Shares of 1p each in the capital of the Company (“the Shares”) were allotted on 13 March 2025 pursuant to the offer for subscription (“the Offer”) contained in the prospectus issued by the Company dated 10 December 2024. The Shares were issued at offer prices based on an unaudited net asset value of 81.80 pence per share ranging from 81.80 pence to 87.50 pence.

Application has been made for the admission of the Shares to the Official List of the Financial Conduct Authority and to trading on London Stock Exchange plc’s market for listed securities on or around 17 March 2025.

In total the Company has allotted 29,484,950 Ordinary Shares to date under the Offer. Following this allotment there are now 301,264,203 Ordinary Shares of 1p each in issue.

For further information please contact:

Gary Fraser, Foresight Group: 020 3667 8181

DB cuts Hugo Boss to 'hold' on muted current trading
data.description:
** Deutsche Bank cuts German fashion group Hugo Boss to "hold" from "buy" as it sees negative comments on current trading outweighing the strong underlying Q4 results

** Hugo Boss CEO Daniel Grieder said the company expected a "muted" Q1 performance, trending "somewhat below" its FY revenue guidance range

** DB says the comments on current trading were "clearly negative", with sales down mid-single-digit percentages

** "It remains to be seen whether the company can deliver improvements in 2025," the broker adds

** It points to softening U.S. consumer confidence as a risk since the market has been the strongest growth driver for Hugo Boss over the last two years

** Hugo Boss is down 0.5%, paring losses after falling as much as 4.3%

Update: Market Chatter: BP to Market 50% Strategic Stake in Solar Unit
data.description:
(Updates to add BP's statement)

BP (BP.L) is gearing up to sell half of its solar unit for cash and a commitment for future investments, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a March sales document to had seen.

The British oil and gas company will accept initial and non-binding proposals for Lightsource bp until June as part of the sales process for the so-called project Scala. The first screening of the bidders is expected in July.

The document specified that BP is looking to partner with "established leaders with extensive experience" in the renewables industry, the report added. BP told Reuters the sale process will be launched soon.

In an emailed statement to MT Newswires, BP said it planned to bring in a partner for Lightsource bp and launch a sale process "in the near future" but declined to comment further.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.description:

** VANQ recorded adj. pretax loss of 34.8 mln pounds ($45 mln) in FY24 vs profit of 17.3 mln pounds in 2023

** Co posts FY net interest income of 420 mln pounds, down 5% from 2023

** Impairment charges in 2024 also increased by 15% to 191 mln pounds vs 165.5 mln pounds in 2023

** In 2024, stock down ~65.56%

($1 = 0.77 pounds)

data.title: Li Auto 4Q Rev CNY44.27B Vs. CNY41.73B >LI
U.K. Fiscal Drive Will Boost Flagging Economy — Market Talk
data.description: The U.K. can expect an upward trajectory this year as increased government spending filters through to the economy, ING economist James Smith tells clients in a note. Britain's GDP unexpectedly contracted in January as production fell back. But that should change as the year goes on and the U.K. Treasury spends more day-to-day, and even if the government decides to pare back its spending plans a little, Smith says. "Given much of that spending boost will land in wages, the impact on the wider economy should be discernable in the GDP numbers this year," he says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)
